rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The procedural issues have not been cleared: 1. Supplement filed 8/10/23 states that petitioner is asking the court to determine the 50% of interest in the real property subject to this petition passing to the surviving spouse's trust; however, this petition does not allow said bypass. Petition may need to seek "passing to the surviving spouse's estate" not to his Trust. the surviving spouse's estate. 2. Proposed Order submitted does not reflect the above issue. rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NISSIM LANYADOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/20/23 for CIU report to be completed (Conservator has not been available) and also due to Counsel's availability. Other issues that will need to be addressed prior to 11/20/23: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing has been filed. (Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) (3) The accounting is for the period 3/30/19 - 12/31/19 only. This second, biennial accounting period should have been for the period ending 3/29/21. Notwithstanding this Court's local rule requiring interim accountings to be for no more than three years, in light of the many delays and concerns raised by this inadequate accounting, the accounting must be amended to make the ending date 3/29/23 (or 12/31/22). (4) The first accounting showed $11,769.41 in cash on hand. This accounting begins with just $11,694.34. (5) The Summary of Account does not balance. (6) The detailed schedules supporting the summary of account must show the nature or purpose of each receipt or disbursement, the source of the receipt or name of the payee, and the date thereof. [Prob. C. Sect. 1062.] The disbursements schedule raises many questions and is entirely inadequate. Receipts must be filed. (7) If an accounting is presented, ORIGINAL bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (8) The assigned examiner has been unable to locate online the order settling the first accounting. Counsel should provide a copy to the assigned examiner at least two weeks before 11/20/23. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (an amended second account, the proof of service or notice of the amended account, declaration regarding the supporting bank statement(s), etc.) must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/8/23.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Still no proof of service of a correct, complete Notice of Hearing of the accounting petition has been filed. (2) No original financial institution statements have been filed. (The 344-pp declaration filed on 5/24/23 purports to attach "computer-generated printouts of original verified electronic statements printed without alterations," but the attachments appear to be just the accounting schedules again, not bank or other financial institution statements. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL BARBARA HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/11/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 07/12/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Is the estate property the decedent's separate property, her one-half share of community property, or both? 3) The cash on hand is exactly the same as the inventory and appraisal value. Has the petitioner not opened an interest-bearing account under the name of the estate, as required by Probate Code section 9652? 4) The cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. A proof must be filed to confirm that the uninsured portion has been transferred to at least two different financial institutions in observance of the FDIC limit or has been invested as authorized by section 9730. 5) No timely election was filed under section 13502 to probate the decedent's property otherwise passing to the petitioner as the surviving spouse without administration (see section 13500). If the petitioner intends to request an order allowing a late election under section 13502(b), the petition must be amended to request that relief (or a separate ex parte petition must be filed). The election must include the statement required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5)("the statement must clearly state that he/she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees.")

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HELEN CHOI PING CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed yet. The proposed order and courtesy copies must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/6/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HING YIP LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need further allegation for the court to be able to grant the petition. The description on #5 of the declaration appears insufficient for the court to declare someone as deceased. 2. Need further allegation or statement as to the date, time and place of death enabling the court to order. 3. Proposed order to be submitted.
